_meta.description: Regulated professions in Quebec that require membership in a professional order (ordre professionnel) before you can legally practice. Critical for anyone in a regulated field.

title_en: Regulated Professions — Professional Orders in Quebec
title_fr: Professions réglementées — Ordres professionnels du Québec
plain_language_summary: Quebec has 46 professional orders (ordres professionnels) that regulate specific occupations. If your profession is regulated, you MUST be a member of your order before practicing — even as a self-employed person. Practicing a regulated profession without authorization is illegal and can result in significant fines. If your field is on this list, membership in your order is your very first step — before registering anything else.
critical_warning: If you are in a regulated profession, you cannot legally practice or charge clients for these services until you are authorized by your professional order. This applies to self-employed work, consulting, and freelancing — not just salaried employment.

how_to_check.tool: Conseil des professions du Québec (CPQ) — official directory of all 46 orders
how_to_check.instruction: Search your profession in the CPQ directory. If it appears, you must join that order before practicing.

common_regulated_professions.note: Software engineers often don't realize they may need OIQ membership if they practice engineering. Purely coding/development work may not require it — verify with OIQ for your specific role.

common_regulated_professions.membership_cost: Varies — annual permit to practice required
common_regulated_professions.note: Must have CPA designation to use the title or offer services requiring CPA authorization

common_regulated_professions.order: Barreau du Québec / Chambre des notaires
common_regulated_professions.note: Must be called to the Bar or be a notary to practice law in Quebec
Must be OAQ member to use the title 'architect' or offer architectural services
Must be OPQ member to use the title 'psychologist' or practice psychology

common_regulated_professions.order: Ordre des infirmières et infirmiers du Québec (OIIQ)
common_regulated_professions.note: Regulated nursing practice requires OIIQ registration

common_regulated_professions.order: Ordre professionnel de la physiothérapie du Québec (OPPQ)
common_regulated_professions.note: Must be OPPQ member to practice physiotherapy
Highly regulated — must be OPQ member

Acupuncture is regulated (Ordre des acupuncteurs du Québec). Naturopathy and homeopathy are NOT regulated — you can practice without an order but cannot claim regulated titles. Verify your specific practice.

common_regulated_professions.order: Not an ordre professionnel — but requires vocational diploma (DEP)
common_regulated_professions.note: Hairdressing is not regulated by a professional order in Quebec, but salons and stylists may need municipal permits. A DEP in hairdressing is required to work in a licensed salon.

common_regulated_professions.order: Organisme d'autoréglementation du courtage immobilier du Québec (OACIQ)
common_regulated_professions.note: Must be licensed by OACIQ to act as a real estate broker in Quebec

immigrant_credential_recognition.plain_language: If you obtained your professional credentials outside Quebec or Canada, you may need to have them recognized before you can join a Quebec professional order. This process varies by profession and country of origin.
immigrant_credential_recognition.process: Contact your specific professional order — they each have their own credential recognition process

support_organization.name: Ordre des professions du Québec / MIFI
support_organization.note: MIFI provides support for immigrants navigating credential recognition

immigrant_credential_recognition.timeline: Can take 6 months to 2+ years depending on profession and country of origin
immigrant_credential_recognition.bridging_programs: Some professions have bridging programs for foreign-trained professionals — check with your order

cost_range.exam_fees: Some orders require qualifying exams — typically $200-$600
cost_range.note: Professional order fees are tax-deductible as business expenses

common_mistakes: Providing services under a regulated title without being a member of the relevant order — illegal and can result in fines
Thinking you don't need order membership for freelance/self-employed work — the obligation applies regardless of employment type
Not checking credential recognition requirements before planning your business timeline
Using a title like 'engineer', 'architect', or 'psychologist' in marketing without being a member of the order — title protection is strict in Quebec
